structure.affirmative: Subject + V2/V-ed — She worked. / He went.
structure.negative: Subject + didn't + V1 — She didn't work. / He didn't go.
structure.question: Did + Subject + V1? — Did she work? / Did he go?

usage.text: Actions at a specific past time

faq.q: How do I form V2 for irregular verbs?
faq.a: Irregular verbs must be memorised: go→went, see→saw, have→had, come→came. Use a verb table when unsure.

faq.q: When do I use Past Simple vs Past Continuous?
faq.a: Past Simple = a completed action. Past Continuous = an ongoing action interrupted by Past Simple: 'I was reading when she called'.

faq.q: Can Past Simple describe repeated habits?
faq.a: Yes: 'She walked to school every day' (past habit). For emphasis, use 'used to': 'She used to walk to school'.
